title: Minecraft Plugin Project Setup
description: Learn how to set up a modern Minecraft plugin project using IntelliJ IDEA, Maven, and PaperMC.

lessons.title: Setting Up the Environment
lessons.content:
# Setting Up Your Workspace

To start developing plugins, you need the right tools. This lesson covers installing the necessary software and plugins.

## Step 1: Install IntelliJ IDEA
Download the **Community Edition** of IntelliJ IDEA. It is the best code editor for Java/Kotlin development.

## Step 2: Install Minecraft Development Plugin
1. Open IntelliJ and go to **Plugins**.
2. Search for 'Minecraft Development' and install it. This provides templates for Spigot, Paper, and Forge.

## Step 3: Configure the JDK
Minecraft 1.18+ requires **Java 17**. If you don't have it, use the 'Download JDK' option within the project creator.

quiz.question: Which IntelliJ edition is recommended for beginners?
What Java version is required for Minecraft 1.18+?

lessons.title: Your First 'Hello World'
lessons.content:
# Creating a Hello World Plugin

Learn how to send a message to the server console when your plugin enables.

## Step 1: Project Metadata
- **Group ID**: Usually a reversed domain (e.g., `me.yourname`).
- **Artifact ID**: Your project name in lowercase with no spaces.

## Step 2: Logging a Message
Instead of using `Bukkit.getConsoleSender()`, use the built-in logger for cleaner logs.

## Example Code
```java
@Override
public void onEnable() {
    getLogger().info("Hello world! Plugin enabled.");
}
```

quiz.question: What method is called when a plugin is enabled?

title: Riding & Mounting Entities

title: Toggling Features & States
description: Learn how to implement toggles for plugin features, player states, and custom mechanics in Minecraft.
